title: Gacha / Loot Box Probability Calculator
description: Calculate gacha pull probabilities for a given rate and attempts. Shows cumulative odds and pulls to reach 50/90/99%.

labels.targetTitle: Pulls needed to reach target probability

howToUse.label: Enter the win rate
howToUse.description: Use the slider or type the per-pull win rate (%) shown in the game's drop rate table.

howToUse.label: Set the number of pulls
howToUse.description: Enter how many pulls you plan to make. Use the slider (1–500) or type directly for larger numbers.

howToUse.label: Read the results
howToUse.description: The large number shows the probability of winning at least once. The table on the right shows cumulative probabilities at common pull counts.

faq.question: What is the probability of getting at least one 3% item in 10 pulls?
faq.answer: About 26.3%. The formula is 1-(1-0.03)^10 ≈ 0.263. There is still a 73.7% chance of getting nothing in 10 pulls.

faq.question: How many pulls do I need for a 50% chance?
faq.answer: The formula is ceil(log(0.5) / log(1-p/100)). For a 3% rate, that is about 23 pulls. Use the 'pulls needed' panel to see the number for 50%, 80%, 90%, 95%, and 99%.

faq.question: Does each pull have an independent probability?
faq.answer: Yes, this calculator assumes each pull is an independent event with the same fixed probability. Pity systems (guaranteed drops after N misses) are not modeled here — the actual probabilities for those are higher.

faq.question: Why doesn't the chance reach 100% no matter how many pulls I make?
faq.answer: Mathematically, 1-(1-p)^n approaches 100% asymptotically but never reaches it. In practice, games implement pity mechanics (hard pity / soft pity) to guarantee a reward after a certain number of pulls. Check the game's official rules for pity details.
